title.purpose: The day name in the header. The largest, only-bold glyph on screen.

body.purpose: Muscle-button labels. Carries the entire grid.

label.purpose: The 'Nd ago' badge inside each muscle button. Reads as a tag, not prose.

meta.purpose: Tabular-nums date subtitle under the day name when off-today.

motion.name: state-fade
motion.purpose: Background, border, and color transitions on every interactive surface.

motion.name: press-scale
motion.purpose: Active-state transform: muscle buttons scale to 0.97, rest-day pill to 0.98.

motion.name: swipe-glide
motion.purpose: The day-to-day translate when the user releases a swipe gesture.

components.name: Muscle Button — Default
components.description: Resting state of a muscle-group toggle. The primary control of the app.
components.css: .ds-muscle-btn { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.35rem; min-height: 92px; padding: 0.5rem; background: oklch(0.25 0.015 250); border: 2px solid oklch(0.35 0.02 250); border-radius: 12px; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.15s, border-color 0.15s, color 0.15s, transform 0.1s; -webkit-tap-highlight-color: transparent; appearance: none; } .ds-muscle-btn:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-muscle-btn__name { font: 500 1.1rem/1.2 -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif; color: oklch(0.8 0.01 250); transition: color 0.15s; } .ds-muscle-btn__badge { font: 500 0.7rem/1 -apple-system, system-ui, sans-serif; letter-spacing: 0.02em; color: oklch(0.56 0.01 250); transition: color 0.15s; } .ds-muscle-btn__badge--recent { color: oklch(0.5 0.008 250); } .ds-muscle-btn__badge--moderate { color: oklch(0.57 0.05 75); } .ds-muscle-btn__badge--due { color: oklch(0.65 0.11 65); } .ds-muscle-btn__badge--overdue { color: oklch(0.62 0.16 30); }

components.name: Muscle Button — Selected
components.description: The toggled state. Sand-amber fill, accent-text label upgraded to 600 weight.
components.css: .ds-muscle-btn { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.35rem; min-height: 92px; padding: 0.5rem; border-radius: 12px; cursor: pointer; appearance: none; -webkit-tap-highlight-color: transparent; transition: background-color 0.15s, border-color 0.15s, color 0.15s, transform 0.1s; } .ds-muscle-btn:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-muscle-btn--selected { background: oklch(0.72 0.12 70); border: 2px solid oklch(0.72 0.12 70); } .ds-muscle-btn__name { font: 600 1.1rem/1.2 -apple-system, system-ui, sans-serif; color: oklch(0.15 0.02 70); } .ds-muscle-btn__badge { font: 500 0.7rem/1 -apple-system, system-ui, sans-serif; letter-spacing: 0.02em; color: oklch(0.3 0.04 70); }

components.name: Muscle Button — Selected + Conflicted
components.description: Loudest state in the system. Fires when the user selects something that conflicts with recent training.
components.css: .ds-muscle-btn { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.35rem; min-height: 92px; padding: 0.5rem; border-radius: 12px; cursor: pointer; appearance: none; -webkit-tap-highlight-color: transparent; transition: background-color 0.15s, transform 0.1s; } .ds-muscle-btn:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-muscle-btn--selected-warn { background: oklch(0.52 0.14 25); border: 2px solid oklch(0.52 0.14 25); } .ds-muscle-btn__name { font: 600 1.1rem/1.2 -apple-system, system-ui, sans-serif; color: oklch(0.95 0.03 25); } .ds-muscle-btn__badge { font: 500 0.7rem/1 -apple-system, system-ui, sans-serif; letter-spacing: 0.02em; color: oklch(0.75 0.06 25); display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.3rem; } .ds-muscle-btn__badge svg { transform: translateY(-0.5px); }

components.name: Muscle Button — Conflicted (not selected)
components.description: An advisory state: this muscle group conflicts with recent training. Tappable; the user decides.
components.css: .ds-muscle-btn { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.35rem; min-height: 92px; padding: 0.5rem; background: oklch(0.25 0.015 250); border-radius: 12px; cursor: pointer; appearance: none; -webkit-tap-highlight-color: transparent; transition: background-color 0.15s, transform 0.1s; } .ds-muscle-btn:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-muscle-btn--conflicted { border: 2px solid oklch(0.44 0.12 25); opacity: 0.65; } .ds-muscle-btn__name { font: 500 1.1rem/1.2 -apple-system, system-ui, sans-serif; color: oklch(0.62 0.12 25); } .ds-muscle-btn__badge { font: 500 0.7rem/1 -apple-system, system-ui, sans-serif; letter-spacing: 0.02em; color: oklch(0.62 0.12 25); display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.3rem; }

components.name: Rest Day Pill — Default
components.description: Full-width dashed-border pill below the muscle grid. Toggling it dims the grid.
components.css: .ds-rest-day-btn { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 100%; padding: 0.75rem; background: transparent; border: 2px dashed oklch(0.35 0.02 250); border-radius: 12px; color: oklch(0.56 0.01 250); font: 500 1rem/1 -apple-system, system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; appearance: none; -webkit-tap-highlight-color: transparent; transition: background-color 0.15s, border-color 0.15s, border-style 0.15s, color 0.15s, transform 0.1s; } .ds-rest-day-btn:active { transform: scale(0.98); }

components.name: Rest Day Pill — Selected
components.description: Active rest-day state. Dashed→solid transition is itself a state cue.
components.css: .ds-rest-day-btn { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 100%; padding: 0.75rem; border-radius: 12px; font-family: -apple-system, system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; appearance: none; -webkit-tap-highlight-color: transparent; transition: transform 0.1s; } .ds-rest-day-btn:active { transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-rest-day-btn--selected { background: oklch(0.72 0.12 70); border: 2px solid oklch(0.72 0.12 70); color: oklch(0.15 0.02 70); font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; }

components.name: Nav Arrow
components.description: Ghost icon button in the day header. Stepwise day navigation; a secondary affordance to the swipe gesture.
components.css: .ds-nav-arrow { background: none; border: none; color: oklch(0.8 0.01 250); font-size: 2rem; line-height: 1; padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; appearance: none; -webkit-tap-highlight-color: transparent; transition: color 0.15s, background-color 0.15s; } .ds-nav-arrow:active { background: oklch(0.31 0.015 250); } .ds-nav-arrow:disabled { opacity: 0.3; pointer-events: none; }

components.name: Today Target
components.description: The single sand-amber dot-in-circle that returns the user to today. Appears only when off-today.
components.html: <button class="ds-today-btn" aria-label="Go to today"><svg viewBox="0 0 20 20" width="18" height="18" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5"><circle cx="10" cy="10" r="7"/><circle cx="10" cy="10" r="2.5" fill="currentColor" stroke="none"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-today-btn { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background: none; border: none; color: oklch(0.72 0.12 70); padding: 0.35rem; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; appearance: none; -webkit-tap-highlight-color: transparent; transition: background-color 0.15s; } .ds-today-btn:active { background: oklch(0.31 0.015 250); }

narrative.overview: Gym Planner reads like a well-made hand tool: a single, fixed face of clearly-labelled controls, its state visible in a glance, indifferent to mood. The surface is a cool slate dark — not black — and the only warmth in the system is a sand-amber accent that marks 'this is what you just did.' Conflict is a muted iron-oxide red, never an alarm. Age is a slow gradient from cool grey at 'fresh' to embered rust at 'overdue', letting the user feel time without reading numbers. The system rejects everything that calls attention to itself: no glassmorphism, no gradient text, no streaks, no toasts, no celebratory motion, no green. It also rejects the opposite trap — the all-grey 'neutral' SaaS dashboard. Warmth and rust live in the palette deliberately, because the product's signal IS time-since-trained, and time-since-trained is a temperature, not a number.
keyCharacteristics: Dark, cool slate surface — never #000, never warm-grey beige.
One warm accent (sand-amber). Used on ≤1 muscle button per row in the common case.
Conflict and age share a warm hue family (rust → amber → red-orange) so the user reads 'warmth = attention.'
Flat. No shadows, no elevation tokens, no card-in-card. Depth is conveyed by surface-vs-bg lightness alone.
Stable layout: state changes are color, weight, opacity. Never position, size, or order at runtime.
One typeface: the system stack. Numerals are tabular where they appear.

rules.name: The One-Voice Rule
rules.body: Sand Amber is the only fully-saturated color in the resting state of the app. If two muscle groups are selected, two Sand Amber buttons appear; that is the maximum saturation the screen ever carries. Iron Oxide only appears as a consequence of selection (you selected a conflict). Never decorative.

rules.name: The Temperature-as-Time Rule
rules.body: Warmth in the palette always means 'older / due / urgent.' Cool means 'recent / fresh.' Do not introduce a cool warning color or a warm 'fresh' color — the user reads the gradient as a thermometer.

rules.name: The No-Green Rule
rules.body: Green is forbidden in this app. It is the single most common fitness-app cliché and the surest tell of 'generic black + neon green tracker.' Success states are not signaled by hue; they are signaled by the warm accent fill of the action that just succeeded.

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: No second typeface. No serif accent, no monospace block, no display face. The system stack carries every role; differentiation comes from weight (400/500/600) and size (0.7→1.25rem) only.

rules.name: The No-Prose Rule
rules.body: This app has no paragraph copy, no marketing line, no help text, no microcopy beyond labels. If a future feature wants paragraph type, the answer is to remove the feature, not add a body style.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. The only visual feedback for press is a 0.97 transform scale (muscle buttons) or a 0.98 transform scale (rest day) lasting 0.1s — never a shadow expansion, never a glow, never a halo.

rules.name: The No-Glassmorphism Rule
rules.body: No backdrop-filter: blur(...). No translucent panels over busy backgrounds. Surfaces are opaque. The dimmed-surface effect on a rest day's grid (opacity 0.6) is the only translucency in the system, and it is applied to ink, not to glass.

dos: Do keep the muscle-button grid in fixed positions. State changes color, weight, opacity — never position, size, or order at runtime.
Do use Sand Amber (oklch(0.72 0.12 70)) and only Sand Amber as the 'selected' fill. Never substitute another hue; never tint it darker for variety.
Do signal conflict through three reinforcing channels: hue (iron-oxide), opacity (0.65 on the un-selected conflicted state), and the inline warning-triangle icon. Never hue alone.
Do use the OKLCH values in global.css as the source of truth. The frontmatter in DESIGN.md mirrors them; if you tweak a shade, tweak it there and re-extract.
Do keep transitions to color/opacity/transform at 0.15s (state) or 0.1s (press). Use cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1) for the day-swipe translate.
Do use tabular-nums for the date subtitle and the 'Nd ago' badge. Number alignment is the point.
Do preserve the dashed border on the Rest Day pill in its un-selected state. The dashed→solid transition on selection is a deliberate state cue.
donts: Don't introduce green anywhere. Not for 'success,' not for a 'training streak,' not for a chart line. The No-Green Rule is absolute — green is the cliché this product is defined against.
Don't use #000 for the background. The bg is oklch(0.13 0.005 250), slightly cool, never pure black.
Don't add gradient text (background-clip: text) to the day name, the accent number, or anything else. Solid color or no.
Don't add glassmorphism. No backdrop-filter: blur(...), no translucent cards over busy backgrounds.
Don't nest cards. There are no cards to begin with; do not introduce a card to wrap the grid, then a card to wrap each button, then a chip inside the button.
Don't use a side-stripe border (left or right border > 1px in a colored hue). Conflicts are full-border, fills, or icon-tinted, never a stripe down the side.
Don't add a streak counter, XP, badge, or 'great job!' toast. The product is not a coach.
Don't add a confirmation dialog or save button to the toggle action. The toggle IS the save.
Don't introduce a second typeface. The system stack carries every role.
Don't add a shadow, glow, or halo on press. The 0.97 transform scale is the entire press feedback.
Don't disable a muscle button to 'protect' the user from a conflict. Conflicts are advisory; the button stays tappable. Disabling breaks the Stable Layout principle.
Don't add motion that is not state-coupled. No idle pulses, no shimmer, no scroll-driven choreography. The day-swipe translate is the only non-state animation in the system.
